home.title: Design guidelines
home.intro: These guidelines define how to use the Neuroartan name, logo, wordmark, symbol, typography, imagery, language, and public identity assets. They are intended for partners, publishers, developers, collaborators, media references, directories, and approved external use where Neuroartan must remain clear, consistent, and recognizable.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.description: Start here for the purpose of the Neuroartan identity system, public positioning, approved use cases, and the baseline rules for representing the company accurately.

sections.title: Logo
sections.description: Use the official Neuroartan logo files exactly as provided. Preserve the approved proportions, spacing, contrast, and theme-aware behavior.

sections.title: Wordmark
sections.description: Use the Neuroartan wordmark when the company name must be immediately readable. Do not redraw, stretch, crop, outline, recolor, or rebuild the wordmark manually.

sections.title: Symbol
sections.description: Use the symbol only when context already identifies Neuroartan or when space is limited. Keep the symbol isolated, legible, and free from additional effects.

sections.title: Dos and don’ts
sections.description: Follow the approved usage rules. Do not distort assets, imply endorsement, use the identity as a decorative motif, or place the brand inside unrelated systems.

sections.title: Gallery
sections.description: Use the gallery as a reference for clean applications of the Neuroartan identity across digital, editorial, product, documentation, and public presentation contexts.

sections.title: Brand partnerships
sections.description: When Neuroartan appears with another brand, keep balanced spacing, equal visual respect, clear hierarchy, and explicit approval before publication.

sections.title: Typography
sections.description: Use system typography through Neuroartan tokens. The typographic system should be calm, precise, readable, and disciplined across all sizes.

sections.title: Language
sections.description: Describe Neuroartan as a cognitive systems company developing ICOS. Use clear language and avoid hype, metaphysical claims, or generic AI-chatbot framing.

sections.title: Contact
sections.description: For brand permissions, asset review, media use, partnerships, commercial use, or co-branded presentation, request review before publication.

presence.title: About Neuroartan
presence.description: Neuroartan is developing ICOS, a governed cognitive operating system for memory, reflection, continuity, and structured intelligence. The brand must communicate clarity, trust, technical discipline, and calm confidence without exaggerated claims.

logo.title: Logo, wordmark, and symbol
logo.intro: The Neuroartan logo system is the primary identifier for the company and its product ecosystem. Use only official registry-backed files and preserve their spacing, proportions, color behavior, and clarity.

sections.title: Primary logo
sections.description: Use the full Neuroartan logo when the audience needs clear company identification, including websites, press references, partner pages, presentations, public listings, and documentation.

sections.title: Wordmark
sections.description: Use the wordmark when the name must remain readable at small sizes or when the symbol is already present nearby. Keep the wordmark proportion unchanged.

sections.title: Symbol
sections.description: Use the symbol where space is limited or where the surrounding context clearly identifies Neuroartan. The symbol should remain isolated, legible, and unmodified.

sections.title: Clear space
sections.description: Keep generous space around every logo asset. Do not place text, icons, photos, borders, gradients, or interface controls too close to the logo.

sections.title: Incorrect use
sections.description: Do not stretch, crop, recolor, rotate, outline, shadow, pattern-fill, mask, animate, or place the logo on noisy backgrounds that reduce clarity.

fundamentals.title: Brand usage and positioning
fundamentals.intro: Neuroartan brand usage should be accurate, restrained, and consistent. The identity must support a serious cognitive systems company, not a generic AI chatbot, decorative technology brand, or speculative concept.

sections.title: Positioning
sections.description: Describe Neuroartan as a cognitive systems company developing ICOS, a governed cognitive operating system for memory, reflection, continuity, and structured intelligence.

sections.title: Naming
sections.description: Use Neuroartan for the company and ICOS for the cognitive operating system. Do not rename, abbreviate, or merge these identities in public-facing materials.

sections.title: Tone
sections.description: Use language that is clear, calm, precise, and technically credible. Avoid hype, theatrical language, metaphysical claims, or exaggerated promises.

sections.title: Dos
sections.description: Use official files, preserve spacing, identify Neuroartan accurately, respect usage context, and keep the company identity visually secondary when it appears in another brand’s material.

sections.title: Don’ts
sections.description: Do not imply endorsement, use the mark without permission, modify the identity, use Neuroartan assets as your own brand, or make Neuroartan more prominent than the primary brand in unrelated contexts.

sections.title: Partnerships
sections.description: Do not imply partnership, sponsorship, certification, endorsement, or approval unless Neuroartan has explicitly authorized that relationship.

sections.title: Permissions
sections.description: Use public assets for reference, listing, editorial, and approved presentation contexts. For commercial, campaign, merchandising, or co-branded use, request review first.

color-typography.title: Typography and visual system
color-typography.intro: The visual system is minimal, token-governed, and theme-aware. It uses white as the light baseline and black as the dark baseline, with quiet contrast, clean spacing, and precise typography.

sections.title: Theme
sections.description: Use white as the light baseline and black as the dark baseline. Surfaces, cards, and panels should not introduce unnecessary tinted backgrounds or visible border boxes.

sections.title: Typography
sections.description: Use system typography through Neuroartan tokens with disciplined scale, controlled weight, and clear hierarchy. Large type should be purposeful, not decorative.

sections.title: Spacing
sections.description: Use generous spacing to create clarity. Separate sections through rhythm and alignment rather than heavy borders, cards, shadows, or decorative containers.

sections.title: Color
sections.description: Use minimal neutral color. Accent use should remain controlled and functional, never decorative or theatrical.

sections.title: Tokens
sections.description: Use Neuroartan tokens for color, typography, spacing, radius, interaction, and layout. Do not create local visual exceptions when a governed token exists.

digital.title: Digital presentation
digital.intro: Neuroartan digital presentation should be clear, responsive, accessible, and consistent with the broader product and website system. Brand assets must be source-owned and registry-backed.

sections.title: Websites
sections.description: Use the brand consistently across Neuroartan websites, documentation, product pages, profile surfaces, and public landing pages.

sections.title: Interfaces
sections.description: Interface usage should prioritize clarity, reading comfort, stable layout, and restrained motion. Do not use decorative hover movement or theatrical effects.

sections.title: Social profiles
sections.description: Use official assets for profile images, banners, and listings. Keep identity presentation simple, readable, and consistent across platforms.

sections.title: Imagery
sections.description: Use imagery that supports cognitive systems, product clarity, research, design, and public trust. Avoid generic AI clichés, excessive gradients, and noisy symbolic visuals.

sections.title: Asset ownership
sections.description: Icons, logos, and brand images must come from the governed source and synced public registry. Do not recreate assets manually inside page-level folders.

public-assets.title: Public assets and applications
public-assets.intro: Use these assets and references for approved public references, listings, editorial coverage, directories, presentations, and identity discovery. Always preserve the files as provided.

sections.title: Logo files
sections.description: Use official Neuroartan logo files from the public registry. Do not export substitute versions from screenshots or manually recreated artwork.

sections.title: Identity references
sections.description: Use the official name, logo, symbol, and description when referencing Neuroartan in directories, product listings, press materials, or external profiles.

sections.title: Gallery references
sections.description: Showcase brand applications through original Neuroartan examples: logo cards, typography scale, interface views, publication covers, profile imagery, and approved digital compositions.

sections.title: Usage review
sections.description: For partnership, commercial, campaign, merchandising, or co-branded use, request review before publication.

sections.title: Contact
sections.description: For brand permissions, asset review, media use, or public identity questions, contact Neuroartan through the official public support channel.
